title: grindelia pusilla
intro: Grindelia pusilla, also known as gumplant or sticky gailardia, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Grindelia pusilla include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower fever and soothe irritated skin. Grindelia pusilla can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used topically to treat skin conditions and internally to alleviate respiratory problems.
